title: [DO NOT MERGE - WIP] Cirrus: use Ubuntu 22.04 LTS
body:
Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

depends on https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/134

comments.body: And now [the "real fun" begins (log).](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5974831744155648) :grin:

@mtrmac ping, any idea why the `too many collisions` in this PR? See : https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6348867829497856/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--Do-not-build-image-at-all-if-build-false--3

(The linked log seems to be a straightforward repo unavailability; looking at https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5492536713150464/logs/main.log instead.)

- Unlikely hypothesis:
  - The test is supposed to generate random `lockPath` names in `PodmanTestCreateUtil`; maybe the RNG is being set up by Ginkgo to always generate the same names. That could be verified by adding logging of the `lockPath` value. (If the generated names are truly random, I have absolutely no idea what’s going on. If they aren’t random, that’s not a _problem_ per se, just a precondition for the hypothesis.)
  - The design requires each test to cleanup up the remote socket after itself when done, so at least assuming there are not 1000 tests running in parallel, an available path should exist even if the names are deterministic. Typically that seems to happen via `podmanTest.Cleanup()` → `StopRemoteService`. Is that cleanup missing in some situations?
  - Even assuming the cleanup is missing or not performed, it is seriously surprising to me that the test log shows a failure on just the 16th test, when there are supposed to be 1000 attempts. Is the test somehow running on a pre-existing directory with close to 1000 lock files already? (And is the RNG seed consistent across runs?)
- Alternative hypothesis: The `lockFile` creation is failing for some other, consistent, reason, maybe a permission error; so a single attempt to create the lock file runs out of all 1000 attempts. Logging the errors returned by `os.OpenFile(lockPath)` would reveal that. Guessing, maybe the current directory (I don’t even know what that directory is) is not writable.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich*, *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

[int podman fedora-36 root container](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6011791649013760) is hitting a `1:30` timeout set in .cirrus.yml so there's something happening that's seriously affecting test performance.  Unfortunately by timing out before completion Cirrus is skipping collection of a bunch of logs that would be helpful to see.  It might be worth running this test manually through `hack/get_ci_vm.sh` (by literally executing `hack/runner.sh`).  This will allow inspection of the environment state and timing data reported at the end of all tests.  *Something(s)* is/are soaking up somewhere around 30-45 minutes of unexpected time.

The RNG/name-collision problem was seen in Fedora-land last year I believe.  Miloslav and I (mostly Miloslav) spent quite a bit of time poking at it.  I would point out that we do have [a software RNG service (rngd) enabled in the Fedora images](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/blob/main/base_images/fedora_base-setup.sh#L24-L44), but not explicitly enabled in Ubuntu (IIRC).  If there isn't one by default, it's possible this needs to be installed/enabled for Ubuntu.

Note that the collision happens on the _very first_ test that is not skipped, and all subsequent tests consistently fail as well. This is not like the random collisions of earlier, when there would be a random failure once in a month.

At this point I’m _guessing_ there’s nothing random about that; the lock file creation is consistently failing for a reason that should be fixable. (But I also didn’t do any work to diagnose this further.)

> Note that the collision happens on the very first test that is not skipped, and all subsequent tests consistently fail as well. This is not like the random collisions of earlier, when there would be a random failure once in a month.

I remember things differently (and maybe wrongly), a `fmt.print()` showed every test basically starting with the same random seed and therefore generating the exact same filenames.  I believe the underlying issue had something to do with using the seed from ginkgo instead of c/common (or something to that effect)?  Anyway, I also remember there were a number of PRs that went out, I just don't can't recall what was fixed or where :disappointed:

Hmmm, so adding `rngd` to Ubuntu doesn't seem to have resolved the issue.  [At least for `int remote ubuntu-2204 root host`](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5201158884032512/html/int-remote-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html).  @mtrmac would you suggest @lsm5 instrument [the socket generating code](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/aea263ef8a0677ac226d7793f4d9c174f3198e80/test/e2e/common_test.go#L303-L323), maybe getting us more detail about what `err` actually is?

e.g. I wonder if we're loosing `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR` (it's empty) and the real problem is a `permission denied` or `file/directory doesn't exist` kind of error.
Yes, that’s what the “alternative hypothesis” part of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14397#issuecomment-1141486843 suggests. Do y’all need me to prepare a patch to that effect?

> Yes, that’s what the “alternative hypothesis” part of [#14397 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14397#issuecomment-1141486843) suggests. Do y’all need me to prepare a patch to that effect?

na, looking into it now. Let me get back to you.

@lsm5 as per https://paste.centos.org/view/f7b8a0f8 I'm onboard with [mtrmac](https://github.com/mtrmac).  Damn lock file simply isn't being created for some reason specific to Ubuntu :confused: 

I peeked at the other failures here and TBH I'd just ignore them until the lockfile thing is figured out.  Hopefully by then there won't be too many more new problems (and maybe less if we're luck) :grin:
@lsm5 looking better now.  The next-worse problem seems to be the ` [Fail] Podman manifest [It] authenticated push` in both Fedora and Ubuntu.

> @lsm5 looking better now. The next-worse problem seems to be the ` [Fail] Podman manifest [It] authenticated push` in both Fedora and Ubuntu.

rerunning them now. Could it be because registry was down or something?

```
[+0673s] Error: pod 11cad2f5b91a006055ed097c6527926a5e061b4829380627e6443a79b4659be2 not found in database: no such pod
[+0673s] Error: pod 02c775b342c144cd0d780e6cec8c160f466fd633f0dc7199c435432891136f96 not found in database: no such pod
[+0673s] Error: pod 267e1676159880afb3444c97398a1f6cbb3d04e5b23d56955037c3afd21446cc not found in database: no such pod
```

> Could it be because registry was down or something?

IIRC this is a locally run registry container.  But yeah, it could be a flake, worth checking open-issues if it reproduces.

> not found in database: no such pod

Yeah, that's concerning.
force-push: Rebased on main.

Interesting debugging data-point:  I used `hack/get_ci_vm.sh int podman ubuntu-2204 root host` and edited `test/e2e/manifest_test.go:296` to use `FIt()` (focus-mode) and the `authenticated push` test passed on the first (and only) run!  So perhaps some other test is screwing something up on the system that causes it to fail (and also on Ubuntu remote).

> Interesting debugging data-point: I used `hack/get_ci_vm.sh int podman ubuntu-2204 root host` and edited `test/e2e/manifest_test.go:296` to use `FIt()` (focus-mode) and the `authenticated push` test passed on the first (and only) run! So perhaps some other test is screwing something up on the system that causes it to fail (and also on Ubuntu remote).

I tried this on 5 tests together along with the authenticated push one, and all seemed to pass. It's only when *all* tests are run that this seems to fail. Might be better to get some other eyes on this.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL.  We're having a major problem with the new Ubuntu and updated Fedora images.  The longer this PR remains un-merged, the higher the changes additional breaking issues will make it into `main` :disappointed: Here's a summary of what we need help with:

* `int podman fedora-36 root container` - [**Many** tests are failing with a 90-second timeout error (log)](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6238864456024064?logs=main#L2249).  This is causing the entire cirrus-task to timeout, meaning we loose a lot of  other useful state/logging information (including the annotated logs).  Something new in this Fedora image must be fundamentally broken for podman-in-podman WRT current podman code.  This is bad and reproducible.
* `int remote fedora-36 root host` - [The `Podman build [It] Remote build .containerignore filtering embedded directory` test fails (log)](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4550014595760128/html/int-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html), also a timeout of some sort.  This is reproducible.
* `int podman ubuntu-2204 root host` - [The `Podman manifest [It] authenticated push` test fails (log)](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/6696261293178880/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--authenticated-push--1).  This same registry image is used on the old Ubuntu 2110, Fedora, and new Fedora images for integration testing, without this failure.  Clearly something is borkt either with the image or the new Ubuntu environment, we just have no clue what it could be.  This problem is reproducible, but only if running all tests.  Perhaps some other test isn't cleaning up properly?

re: authenticated push
> dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5868: connect: no route to host

That is weird, most likely the loopback interface is down, check `ip addr show dev lo`

re: 90 sec timeout in container test

It looks like all timeouts are in the podman checkpoints tests, so I suspect an issue with that.

> That is weird, most likely the loopback interface is down, check ip addr show dev lo

I should think that would show up by other tests failing then, no?  Reminder: It only breaks when we run all the tests, if we isolate `authenticated push` it passes no problem :confused:

Anyone have a guess on:  `int remote fedora-36 root host` - [The Podman build [It] Remote build .containerignore filtering embedded directory test fails (log)](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4550014595760128/html/int-remote-fedora-36-root-host.log.html), also a timeout of some sort. This is reproducible.

> > That is weird, most likely the loopback interface is down, check ip addr show dev lo
> 
> I should think that would show up by other tests failing then, no? Reminder: It only breaks when we run all the tests, if we isolate `authenticated push` it passes no problem confused

Well only if other tests also use 127.0.0.1. I think only some network tests require this. Maybe they are skipped in the container tests or some other test sets the lo down due some weird bug.

a couple of observations RE: authenticated push failures

- the failure occurs because of something in `login_logout_test.go` conflicting with the test.

- the failure to ping registry occurs even though `ping localhost` works and `ip addr show dev lo` shows the interface up 
```
# ip addr show dev lo
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN group default qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```

@cevich If I use the same registry startup code for `authenticated push` as the one in login_logout_test.go, the authenticated push will consistently pass. I haven't yet been able to narrow things down further.

@vrothberg wdyt? should/can the registry startups be unified? Or is there good reason behind them being separate?

Well, your [f35 failure](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5093701889294336/html/int-remote-fedora-35-root-host.log.html) is the unpleasant new #14653 flake. f36 remote looks like a flake too, I've restarted it.

[ubuntu local](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/4530751935873024/html/int-podman-ubuntu-2204-root-host.log.html#t--authenticated-push--1) looks like a networking problem:
```
         # podman [options] push --tls-verify=false --creds=userZqcY:XdNYDe1NtY9jbZ0 --format=v2s2 localhost:5894/alpine:latest
         Getting image source signatures
         time="2022-06-17T14:18:13Z" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 1s ... (1/3). Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:03901b4a2ea88eeaad62dbe59b072b28b6efa00491962b8741081c5df50c65e0 at destination: pinging container registry localhost:5894: Get \"http://localhost:5894/v2/\": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5894: connect: no route to host"
         Getting image source signatures
         time="2022-06-17T14:18:26Z" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 1s ... (2/3). Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:03901b4a2ea88eeaad62dbe59b072b28b6efa00491962b8741081c5df50c65e0 at destination: pinging container registry localhost:5894: Get \"http://localhost:5894/v2/\": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5894: connect: no route to host"
         Getting image source signatures
         time="2022-06-17T14:18:38Z" level=warning msg="Failed, retrying in 1s ... (3/3). Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:03901b4a2ea88eeaad62dbe59b072b28b6efa00491962b8741081c5df50c65e0 at destination: pinging container registry localhost:5894: Get \"http://localhost:5894/v2/\": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5894: connect: no route to host"
         Getting image source signatures
         Error: trying to reuse blob sha256:03901b4a2ea88eeaad62dbe59b072b28b6efa00491962b8741081c5df50c65e0 at destination: pinging container registry localhost:5894: Get "http://localhost:5894/v2/": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5894: connect: no route to host
```
What's weird is, if localhost networking were broken, I would expect a LOT more tests to fail. Nope, it's only this one test. And it's not a port conflict, because three retries get three different ports. @Luap99 any suggestions on how to find out more info in this case?

@edsantiago right, the ubuntu failure looks like a registry conflict between `login_logout_test.go` and `manifest_test.go`. I tried running the authenticated push from `login_logout_test.go` by not running a new registry, and it worked fine.

> What's weird is, if localhost networking were broken, I would expect a LOT more tests to fail. Nope, it's only this one test. And it's not a port conflict, because three retries get three different ports. @Luap99 any suggestions on how to find out more info in this case?

see https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14397#issuecomment-1155587234

`no route to host` is a very weird error, I guess if this is a container with forwarded ports this can happen when the container ip is not reachable. Why this happens no idea, most likely some conflict/cleanup error. Can you get the `iptables -nvL -t nat` output when this happens?

>the ubuntu failure looks like a registry conflict

@lsm5 I don't understand what that means. No such conflict should be possible. I think @Luap99 's suggestion is worth pursuing: can you instrument the test to run (and display the output of) the `iptables` command he suggests? Running it immediately before the failing podman commands (which seem consistent) is much easier than running it on failure.

@lsm5 slightly newer images: `c4514861978746880` you could try (from #14719), but I'm not hopeful there will be much difference.  I tried to make it clear in my PR that we need to get this one fixed & merged first.  What problems are outstanding here, that you're not currently working on (so I can help with them in parallel)?

> @lsm5 slightly newer images: `c4514861978746880` you could try (from #14719), but I'm not hopeful there will be much difference. I tried to make it clear in my PR that we need to get this one fixed & merged first. What problems are outstanding here, that you're not currently working on (so I can help with them in parallel)?

@cevich pushed a change with the new id. RE: outstanding problems, the same ones as before I guess. `authenticated push` and the `90s` timeout. Last week I was occupied with RHEL and I'm being asked to spend more time on arm CI. So, if anyone else wants to take this over, please feel free.

> Last week I was occupied with RHEL and I'm being asked to spend more time on arm CI. So, if anyone else wants to take this over, please feel free.

I hear ya...these image-update PRs are a major time-sink and always need lots of hand-holding and teamwork.  Speaking of which, I s'pose there's no reason other maintainers can't just force-push to your branch to try and kick this PR down the road.

Yay, one down, only two issues left by my counting:

* `Podman manifest [It] authenticated push`  (mostly on F35 + U2204)
* [Fail] Podman build [It] Remote build .containerignore filtering embedded directory (only on 'remote' tests)
Update: `Podman build` (remote) only failed on my #14719 but not here, so that must be a flake.  Meaning only `authenticated push` needs debugging/fixing :birthday:

I think there's something broken in networking. I did a `re-run with console`, and while tests were running:
```console
# hack/podman-registry start
PODMAN_REGISTRY_IMAGE="quay.io/libpod/registry:2.6"
PODMAN_REGISTRY_PORT="5055"
PODMAN_REGISTRY_USER="user0cDX"
PODMAN_REGISTRY_PASS="keyT0ZSieGrqCy3"
# telnet localhost 5055
Trying 127.0.0.1...
telnet: Unable to connect to remote host: No route to host
```
`lsof` on the process shows it listening:
```
conmon  178016 root    5u     IPv4             706971      0t0     TCP *:5055 (LISTEN)
```
There is a bad interface on the system:
```console
# ip a
...
3: cni-podman0: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP group default qlen 1000
    link/ether 12:d0:4c:e4:e6:c3 brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.88.0.1/16 brd 10.88.255.255 scope global cni-podman0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::10d0:4cff:fee4:e6c3/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
```
Deleting it (via `ip link del`) makes the port accessible again. So, something seems to be creating the old-style CNI interface somehow. But what?

@Luap99 here's the iptables output you requested:

That clearly shows a mix use of cni/netavark which is not supported!
It looks to me like that something is calling the systems podman which uses cni and not ./bin/podman. Or they do not use the correct --network-backend option that is set by the e2e tests.

>Or they do not use the correct --network-backend option that is set by the e2e tests.

Thanks you, that's it.

`manifest_test.go` is the only test that uses `hack/podman-registry-go`:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/cf747399b13432d000cfd9556a248681363dda2d/test/e2e/manifest_test.go#L297-L300

(side note: the double use of `registry` here is horrible: it's both a constant and a variable. ~~Reminder to self, fix that~~ Submitted #14834 to fix).

Anyhow, `hack/podman-registry-go` invokes `hack/podman-registry`, which runs `bin/podman` but has no way of knowing the magic options used in e2e tests.

SOLUTION: get rid of the `hack/podman-registry-go` stuff. Just run the registry however all the other tests do it.
Submitted #14845 to address the registry startup issue. I chose not to heed my "however all the other tests do it" declaration because that way lies madness.

@lsm5: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

> because that way lies madness.

Lol, thanks for checking into this Ed and Paul.  FYI: I'm pretty sure during setup we `make install` so the system podman ***should*** be the same as `./bin/podman`.  However, I cannot predict what evil lurks in `/etc/containers/*` or the runtime database, so it's easily possible something causes it to revert to CNI.

>during setup we make install

That doesn't matter. The short version of the problem is: e2e tests make excessive use of podman command line args (`podman --this --that --oh --my --gosh --so --many --options`), hence, anything that invokes plain `podman` (like the registry script) is gonna screw up the system.

But it's moot, because my fixer PR is merged, so, @lsm5, please rebase & repush, this should pass now.

> hence, anything that invokes plain podman (like the registry script) is gonna screw up the system.

Well screw up any future e2e runs, yes maybe.  Though `--despite --so --many --options`, all the e2e tests still share some resources, disk space, lots of networking aspects, firewall rules, etc.  So we'll never fully be able to isolate them.  In any case, at least for e2e maybe it would be be useful to link `/usr/bin/podman` to `/bin/false` instead of installing:  To help make this class of problem more obvious if it ever creeps back in later.  Just a random idea, and an easy change to implement.

>Well screw up any future e2e runs

No, I mean, screw up the e2e test run right smack in the middle of the run. It's kind of complicated, but here's the skeleton:
* e2e tests run, blah blah, yeah, going fine
* one of the e2e tests invokes a script that runs plain `podman`, not `podman --with --all --the --options`
* ker-blammo. Everything gets destroyed because plain podman switches the system from netavark to CNI and all goes to hell

Does that make sense?

> Does that make sense?

Oh I see, gotcha, okay thanks.

I'm going to close this PR in favor of: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14719 because:

1. Lokesh is busy with other things
2. Having multiple image update PRs open is confusing
3. We are in a holding-pattern for new criu and runc packages getting into new images anyway

Aw, phooey, @cevich, can I ask you to reconsider? All this needs is a rebase & push, and life will (should) be happy. We can get Ubuntu 22 right now, all we need is this PR to merge. The criu issue does not affect this PR.

I think all we're loosing is the PR comments (which may actually be valuable).  My #14719 has all the changes here + the fix for bats (slightly newer images) + my attention  - @lsm5 asked to be let off the hook in a "help!" comment above.

Otherwise I'm not strongly opposed to re-opening this and re-running the tests, just lazy and don't want to be overwhelmed with too many image-update PRs in-flight.
